retrieved_docs.title: Narcotic Bowel Syndrome -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: IBS: Abdominal pain, flatulence, bloating, diarrhea/constipation, mucus in stools Gastritis: burning pain in mid epigastrium, nausea, vomiting, anorexia, bloating, belching, and weight loss Chronic pancreatitis: persistent or intermittent mid-epigastric pain that may radiate to the back, bulky, foul-smelling stools, greasy stools, and weight loss Biliary colic: intermittent pain, nausea and vomiting, often worsened with fatty foods Gallstones: RUQ pain, nausea, vomiting but there may be pain relief with opioids Peptic ulcer disease: mid-epigastric pain that is worse on an empty stomach and decreased pain with a meal. Thus, تنظير مريئي is classified as a subtype of التنظير الداخلي للجهاز الهضمي and is included among other gastrointestinal diagnostic procedures, highlighting its role in the systematic assessment of esophageal pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 2 -- Community 447
retrieved_docs.content: Other gastrointestinal diagnostic procedures represent a broader category that includes تنظير مريئي (esophageal endoscopy). The procedure التنظير الداخلي للجهاز الهضمي (gastrointestinal endoscopy) serves as the parent category for both تنظير مريئي and تنظير الجهاز الهضمي (digestive system endoscopy). This hierarchical organization shows that esophageal endoscopy and general digestive system endoscopy are specific types of gastrointestinal endoscopy, which itself is part of the wider group of other gastrointestinal diagnostic procedures. Understanding these relationships helps clinicians classify and select appropriate endoscopic techniques for evaluating the gastrointestinal tract.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 2621
retrieved_docs.content: Esophagoscopy for injection of esophageal varices is an endoscopic procedure specifically employed in the treatment of دوالي المَريء (esophageal varices), linking the diagnostic and therapeutic technique directly to this vascular condition of the esophagus.
